title: prunella vulgaris subvar. surrecta
intro: Prunella vulgaris subvar. surrecta, also known as self-heal, is a plant species belonging to the mint family and is characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of self-heal include its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of self-heal include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and aid in wound healing and skin issues. Self-heal can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
